title: Stop Chatting, Start Building
subtitle: Your agent is an operating system, not a chatbot. Here's how to install the apps that make it useful.

sections.heading: The Mindset Shift
sections.content: Stop treating your agent like a search engine. It's a builder. Give it tools, not just questions.

sections.heading: What Skills Actually Are
sections.content: Skills are capabilities you add to your agent: file access, web browsing, code execution, API calls, database queries.

sections.heading: The 6 Categories
sections.content: Communication, Research, Creation, Analysis, Management, Integration. Know which you need.

sections.heading: The 4 Tools Every Agent Needs
sections.content: File system access. Web search. Code execution. Memory/note-taking.

sections.heading: How to Pick Skills Safely
sections.content: Check the developer. Read permissions. Sandbox first. Start with official/verified skills.

sections.heading: Skills vs. System Prompts vs. Memory Files
sections.content: Skills = what it can do. System prompts = how it behaves. Memory = what it knows. All three work together.

sections.heading: Your First 30 Minutes
sections.content: Install 3 core skills. Test each one. Set up a simple workflow. See the difference.

sections.heading: What Changes
sections.content: With the right skills, your agent goes from answering questions to completing tasks. That's the real unlock.

key_takeaways: Your agent is an operating system — skills are the apps that make it useful
Every agent needs 4 core tools: file access, web search, code execution, memory
Skills define what it can do, system prompts define behavior, memory defines what it knows
